title: Fix the JavaScript Trie Node Inserter

statement.markdown: Implement a function that inserts a word into a Trie data structure. The function should return `true` if the word is successfully inserted, and `false` otherwise. The word may contain duplicate letters, and the Trie may contain existing words. Note that the function should handle the case where the word is already present in the Trie.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the word to be inserted into the Trie.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the word was successfully inserted.
statement.notes: The Trie is represented as a nested object, with each node containing a `children` property that maps characters to child nodes. The function should handle the case where the word is empty.

constraints.description: The maximum length of the word

examples.explanation: An empty word cannot be inserted into the Trie.
The word 'apple' is successfully inserted into the Trie.
The word 'apple' is already present in the Trie, so it is not inserted again.

hints.text: First hint text: Consider how to handle the case where the word is already present in the Trie.
